mainEntity.name: What if I had a bad back before this job? Can I still claim occupational disease?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if your job "aggravated" or worsened your pre-existing condition, it is covered under workers' compensation. You are entitled to benefits for the "new" level of disability caused by your work activities.
acceptedAnswer.description: Utah and Idaho laws are designed to protect you even if you were already prone to a certain condition. If your job "lighted up" or "accelerated" a dormant condition, it is fully compensable. For example, if you had mild back issues that stayed manageable until you started a heavy-lifting job that caused a herniated disc, the insurance company is responsible. They will fight this by trying to say it's 100% pre-existing. We use medical records to show your "baseline" before the job and how your function has declined since the exposure. Don't let a "pre-existing" label scare you—it is one of the most common myths used to stop people from filing.
